title: Delicate Australian Fish Sushi
description: A pungent and chewy sushi dish that showcases Australian fish, bread rolls, and sashimi-cut ingredients, making it fodmap-friendly.

ingredients.name: Hapuku Fish Fillets
ingredients.notes: Fresh and sustainable Australian Hapuku fish fillets.

ingredients.name: Cucumber
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced and seeded for digestion.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced and peeled for easy digestion.

ingredients.name: Avocado
ingredients.notes: Mashed and ripe for a creamy texture.

ingredients.name: Rice Vinegar
ingredients.notes: Gluten-free and organic for a tangy flavor.

ingredients.name: Chives
ingredients.notes: Fresh and finely chopped for garnish.

instructions: Prepare the Hapuku fish fillets by slicing them into thin sashimi-cut pieces.
Prepare the bread rolls by cutting them into small pieces and toasting them for a crispy texture.
Prepare the cucumber and carrot slices by placing them in a bowl of ice-cold water for 10 minutes to remove excess water.
Drain the cucumber and carrot slices and pat them dry with paper towels.
Mash the avocado in a bowl and add the lime juice, rice vinegar, and sesame seeds. Mix well to combine.
Assemble the sushi rolls by placing a piece of fish on a bread roll, followed by a slice of cucumber, carrot, and avocado mixture.
Roll the sushi tightly but gently and slice it into individual pieces.
Garnish the sushi with chopped chives and serve immediately.
